DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Use Case Definitions

Definitions for documenting when to use and when not to use a design system artifact. Use cases provide scenario-driven guidance that complements guidelines by framing decisions in terms of concrete situations.

Source: common/usecase.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • useCase
  • useCases

useCase

A single use case scenario describing a situation in which an artifact is or is not appropriate.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
descriptionrichTextrequired
alternativeobjectoptionalThe recommended alternative artifact for this scenario. Typically provided for whenNotToUse entries to direct the reader toward a more appropriate component or pattern.
examplesexample[]optionalOptional examples illustrating the use case scenario.

References: richText, example

Example

{
  "description": "When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or opening a dialog.",
  "alternative": {
    "name": "link",
    "rationale": "Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support standard navigation behaviors such as open-in-new-tab. Using a button for navigation creates a mismatch between the announced role and the actual behavior."
  },
  "examples": [
    {
      "title": "Button used for form submission",
      "presentation": {
        "type": "code",
        "code": "<form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>\n  <Button variant=\"primary\">Save changes</Button>\n</form>",
        "language": "jsx"
      }
    }
  ]
}

useCases

Scenario-driven guidance describing when an artifact is and is not the right choice. Complements guidelines by framing decisions around concrete user or developer situations rather than abstract rules.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
whenToUseuseCase[]optionalScenarios in which this artifact is the appropriate choice.
whenNotToUseuseCase[]optionalScenarios in which this artifact should not be used. Each entry SHOULD include an `alternative` pointing to a more appropriate artifact or pattern.

References: useCase

Example

{
  "whenToUse": [
    {
      "description": "When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, opening a dialog, or confirming a decision."
    },
    {
      "description": "When a destructive or irreversible action needs to be initiated, such as deleting a record or revoking access. Pair with a confirmation dialog."
    }
  ],
  "whenNotToUse": [
    {
      "description": "When the action navigates the user to a different page or URL.",
      "alternative": {
        "name": "link",
        "rationale": "Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support standard navigation behaviors such as open-in-new-tab."
      }
    },
    {
      "description": "When the user needs to select one option from a set of mutually exclusive choices.",
      "alternative": {
        "name": "radio-group",
        "rationale": "Radio groups communicate exclusivity through their semantic role. A set of buttons styled to look like a selector does not convey mutual exclusivity to assistive technology."
      }
    },
    {
      "description": "When the only content is an icon with no visible text label.",
      "alternative": {
        "name": "icon-button",
        "rationale": "Icon buttons enforce an aria-label requirement and apply size adjustments for icon-only touch targets. A standard button with its label removed may fail accessibility requirements silently."
      }
    }
  ]
}
↑ Back to top

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification
